title: Per-BIF x-register save audit — complete GC root hardening
purpose: The infrastructure GC hardening (alloc_words_prereserved / alloc_cons_prereserved) eliminated mid-loop GC in list/map allocation helpers. However, individual BIFs that call ensure_heap_space still need to save their input arguments to x-registers before that call, because ensure_heap_space may trigger GC which moves heap objects. Any BIF that holds a reference to a heap-allocated Term (list, tuple, map, binary, closure) in a Rust local variable across an ensure_heap_space call has a stale-pointer bug. lists:reverse was the first fix (landed in 0.4.1). This brief audits every BIF and fixes all remaining sites.
task: Audit every BIF function that calls ensure_heap_space (or any allocation method that may trigger GC) while holding heap-term references in Rust locals. For each site: save args to x-registers before ensure_heap_space, re-read from x-registers after. Document the audit as a checklist so future BIFs follow the pattern.

requirements.title: Audit all stdlib_stubs BIFs
requirements.spec: Audit every function in crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/ that takes &[Term] args and calls ensure_heap_space, alloc_cons, alloc_tuple, alloc_map, alloc_binary, or any other allocation method. For each: if args contain heap-allocated terms and the function calls an allocating method, save args to x-registers before the first allocation, re-read after.
acceptance: Every BIF in collection_bifs.rs saves heap-term args to x-registers before allocation
Every BIF in lists_bifs.rs saves heap-term args to x-registers before allocation
Every BIF in maps_bifs.rs saves heap-term args to x-registers before allocation
Every BIF in io_bifs.rs saves heap-term args to x-registers before allocation
Every BIF in gleam_stdlib_ffi.rs saves heap-term args to x-registers before allocation
No BIF holds a heap-term Rust local across an ensure_heap_space call

requirements.title: Audit gate1/gate2/gate3 BIFs
requirements.spec: Audit BIFs in bifs.rs, process_bifs.rs, and gate3_bifs/ for the same pattern. Arithmetic BIFs that allocate BigInt (after B-173) are especially important — they receive numeric args that may be heap-allocated BigInts.
acceptance: Every BIF in bifs.rs that allocates saves heap-term args first
Every BIF in process_bifs.rs that allocates saves heap-term args first
Every BIF in gate3_bifs/ that allocates saves heap-term args first
No BIF holds a heap-term Rust local across an allocation call

requirements.title: Audit OTP stubs and Gleam FFI BIFs
requirements.spec: Audit BIFs in otp_stubs/ and gleam_ffi.rs for the same pattern. These BIFs often receive complex terms (tuples, lists, binaries) and allocate result terms.
acceptance: Every BIF in otp_stubs/ that allocates saves heap-term args first
Every BIF in gleam_ffi.rs that allocates saves heap-term args first
Every BIF in selector_ffi.rs that allocates saves heap-term args first

requirements.title: Document the GC-safe BIF pattern
requirements.spec: Add a module-level doc comment to crates/beamr/src/native/mod.rs documenting the x-register save pattern for BIF authors. Include: when it's needed (any BIF that holds heap-term args and allocates), how to do it (save to x-regs, ensure_heap_space, re-read), and the reference implementation (bif_lists_reverse in collection_bifs.rs).
acceptance: Module doc in native/mod.rs describes the GC-safe BIF pattern
Pattern includes save-to-x-regs, ensure_heap_space, re-read sequence
References bif_lists_reverse as the canonical example

boundaries: BIFs that only work with small_int or atom args (immediate values, not heap pointers) do not need x-register saves
BIFs that never allocate (pure reads, comparisons) do not need x-register saves
The audit covers existing BIFs only — future BIFs follow the documented pattern
OUT OF SCOPE: Automated compile-time enforcement of the pattern (would require a custom lint)
OUT OF SCOPE: Changing the BIF calling convention to auto-save args
verification: grep -n 'ensure_heap_space' across all BIF files confirms every call is preceded by x-register saves for heap-term args
No BIF function holds heap-term locals (list, tuple, map, binary, closure Terms) across allocation calls
